_comment_what: WASP-43b real-data PRODUCTION retrieval (30 h budget, GH200/A100). Successor to wasp43b_pilot_gpu.json (2026-07-02/03 run, 15.1 h): same data, priors, geometry, and emission model; new forward-model numerics and a fixed mutation kernel. Every choice is documented in retrieval/README.md (the single human-readable doc for this suite).
_comment_provenance: System parameters: Esposito et al. 2017 (A&A 601, A53), the source Bell et al. 2024 used for their fiducial fit: M*=0.688 Msun, R*=0.6506 Rsun, Rp=1.006 Rjup, b=0.689, i=82.109 deg, log g_p=3.696 (g=49.66 m/s^2). Ephemeris: Ivshina & Winn 2022 (ApJS 259, 62), P=0.813474037 d; omega=2*pi/P (tidally locked). a_planet_m is the SHALLOW-WATER SPHERE RADIUS (planet radius, 1.006 Rjup in meters), NOT the orbital semi-major axis. Six inferred parameters: tau_rad, tau_drag, F_p/F_s, Phibar, DPhieq, noise inflation. Planck band wavelengths/weights are injected at run time from observations.npz (written by prepare_wasp43b_observations.py, which now also masks the secondary-eclipse ingress/egress contact windows - see its provenance JSON).
_comment_solver: Semi-implicit gravity-wave scheme + RAW filter + exponential hyperdiffusion at dt=600 s with the DEFAULT K6=1.24e33 (no more 5e33 retune - the implicit solve removes the gravity-wave dt ceiling). Corner-validated 2026-07-02 (scripts/benchmark_new_numerics.py, stage 'corners'): 13/16 prior-box corners stable at these exact settings, including both corners that kill the explicit dt=120/K6=5e33 pilot solver; the 3 failures are the DPhieq/Phibar=2.5 nightside-collapse corners, strongly data-excluded (pilot posterior contrast max 1.96) and soft-rejected by the NaN -> -1e30 likelihood guard. Scheme-level offset vs the explicit pilot: identical hot-spot offset, ~4.6% smaller day-night amplitude - results are conditioned on the SI scheme; do not compare posteriors across schemes without noting this. Mixed precision (f32 dynamics / f64 light curve): forward agrees with pure f64 to 0.006 ppm on a ~4100 ppm curve, gradients to ~1e-4; ~2x GPU speedup is a claim, not load-bearing for the 30 h budget.
_comment_sampler: Pilot post-mortem: one-shot step-size tuning at prior scale -> MALA acceptance collapsed to 0.001 by beta=1 -> only 25 unique particles. Fixes: (1) mcmc_stage_adapt re-tunes the step size after every tempering stage toward accept=0.574 (MALA optimum); (2) the MALA proposal is preconditioned with the weighted per-dimension particle std (diagonal, u-space), so the proposal tracks the posterior as it narrows; (3) N=64 -> 256 particles and 20 -> 30 mutation steps/stage, funded by the ~6x cheaper semi-implicit forward. Watch 'unique=' in the run log: it should stay comparable to N through beta=1.
_comment_budget: Cost model from the pilot (24.5 min/stage at N=64, 20 steps, dt=120): x4 particles, x1.5 steps, /6 semi-implicit forward -> ~25 min/stage, 40-50 stages -> 17-21 h without the mixed-precision speedup, ~9-12 h with it. Walltime 36 h (gpu_long) covers the no-speedup case with margin; per-stage checkpointing (smc_checkpoint.npz) bounds any loss to one stage.
